titleEn: Pig Progress6 Pig reasons not to miss SPACE in 2026Jul 13Article
titleZh: 2026年SPACE展会六大养猪创新亮点：精准饲喂、AI胴体检测与智能称重引领行业变革
summaryZh: 2026年9月法国SPACE畜牧展将颁发32项Innov'SPACE创新奖，其中6项养猪创新获一星认可。亮点包括：Asserva的Materneo 4.0母猪精准饲喂系统，基于行为与采食量实时调整；Yxia的采精台，采用不锈钢与可丽耐材质，易清洁且防菌；Unissia的Pig'Scan AI胴体检测系统，自动识别咬尾、划伤等福利指标；Maison Bleue的Sanihouse病死畜管理设施，实现无接触收集；IMV的Smart Weight智能估重系统，无需称重即可估算仔猪平均体重；IFIP的Foodbasket7营养-环境评估工具，可量化膳食对健康与地球的影响。这些创新聚焦数据驱动、自动化与生物安全，为养猪业降本增效、提升动物福利提供了可落地的技术方案。
translationZh:
今年，SPACE展会将有32项创新获得Innov'SPACE奖。其中26项获得一星，6项获得二星。评审团的首选将在9月15日展会期间的Innov'SPACE活动上揭晓。

以下为展会上的养猪创新项目：

**母猪精准饲喂系统** ⭐
Asserva: Materneo 4.0
Materneo 4.0是法国畜牧设备公司Asserva开发的母猪精准饲喂系统，适用于产房。该系统用动态、个体化的调整取代了理论饲喂计划。饲喂基于母猪的行为、实际采食量和采食能力进行实时控制。系统集成了自动仔猪称重功能，用于测量生长速率。仔猪的体重变化可作为直接指标，在出现可见损失之前修正营养输入。该系统结合了三个模块：分次剂量的个体化分配、动物性能测量以及营养算法。其目标有两个：最大化仔猪在此关键阶段的平均日增重，以及保持母猪体况以优化恢复和未来繁殖性能。每个产床都成为一个由数据驱动的自主决策单元。（8号馆，A21）

**公猪采精台** ⭐
Yxia: Mounting dummy
公猪采精台是用于人工授精采精的重要工具。该产品的目的是让动物舒适地趴在上面，同时保持其阴茎可触及以进行刺激和采精。这款采精台的结构设计独特，因其易于拆卸的部件而便于最佳清洁。它还配有一个单柱、倾斜且偏心的底座，以优化动物阴茎周围的空间，方便操作员移动。材质方面，它采用不锈钢制成——坚固且高度耐腐蚀——其顶部表面由可丽耐（一种树脂与矿物粉末的复合材料）制成。后者无孔且耐冲击。这些创新特性的结合，使得设计出符合人体工学且耐用的采精台成为可能，同时降低了采精过程中的细菌污染风险。（8号馆，B32）

**猪胴体损伤与性别自动检测** ⭐
Unissia – Pig’Scan
Pig’Scan是一款基于人工智能的自动化猪胴体分析系统，旨在监测屠宰场的动物福利、咬尾和健康指标。它执行同步多视角采集（背部、头部和尾部区域），自动量化尾长、咬尾损伤、背部划痕、打斗迹象、运输相关损伤以及某些潜在的虐待动物指标。借助其机器视觉算法和RFID追溯功能，它将以往主观的观察结果转化为可统计使用的数字数据。分析胴体背部可识别出可能揭示与应激、运输、饲养密度或养殖实践相关问题的划痕、撞击和损伤。它提供标准化和可重复的测量、视觉证据的自动存档，并减少人工监测的工作量。（8号馆，A23a）

**简化病死畜管理** ⭐
Maison Bleue: Sanihouse
Sanihouse由Innovlap和Maison Bleue设计，是一种专门用于管理农场病死动物的设施。该设施由隔热、预制的钢筋混凝土构件构成，包含一个带有不锈钢平台和自动化电动门的组件。它使病死畜的移除更加轻松和安全。平台的电动化与门的开启同步，其尺寸可容纳化制桶。这些桶会自动移动到“养殖区”一侧用于装载尸体，或移动到“公共区”一侧，让化制服务商可以完全自主地收集。通过将尸体隔离在易于清洁和消毒的隔热设施中，确保化制服务商无需进入农场范围即可独立进出，并减少人工处理，该系统在保证符合卫生法规的同时，简化了养殖户的病死畜管理。（室外展区，E21）

**自动仔猪体重估算** ⭐
IMV Technologies: Smart Weight
Smart Weight是Smart Counting平台的一个附加功能，专为离开产房的10公斤以下仔猪设计。当动物通过计数通道时，系统会自动在多张图像中追踪它们，汇总视觉测量结果，并估算该批次的平均体重和体重离散度，无需人工称重或绕道至地磅。只需简单的用户校准，无需现场安装，也无需特定的预防性维护。这样，一个计数器就变成了一个客观的生产管理工具：即时、带时间戳的数据，可集成到养殖场管理软件中。它带来了精准度、一致性和一个新的均匀度指标。经济上，它减少了收集时间、运输成本，并支持订阅模式。（1号馆，G76）

**畜牧业营养-环境评估工具** ⭐
IFIP: Foodbasket7
法国猪业研究所（IFIP）将在SPACE展会上推出Foodbasket7：一个免费的网页工具，允许公众深入了解其饮食选择的后果。用户只需几分钟即可创建一个每周食品篮，该工具随后将提供两个互补维度的简单可视化评估：食物的营养质量（通过覆盖28种必需营养素，根据用户年龄、性别和体力活动定义）以及食品篮的环境影响（如温室气体排放、水消耗、土地使用等）。其创新之处在于其全面的方法，允许在完整饮食的尺度上进行推理，而不是基于单个食品项目。对于畜牧业部门，Foodbasket7提供了一个科学分析框架，通过整合食物的营养密度，来评估动物产品对健康、多样化和可持续饮食的贡献。该工具可在IFIP网站上免费获取。（8号馆，A23a）
featuredReason: 该新闻集中呈现了2026年SPACE展会上最具代表性的6项养猪创新，覆盖精准饲喂、AI视觉检测、智能估重、生物安全等关键领域，技术成熟度高且已进入商业化阶段，对智慧养猪从业者具有直接的选型参考和趋势指引价值。
contentFull:
6 Pig reasons not to miss SPACE in 2026

6 pig-specific innovations will be awarded at the upcoming international animal husbandry show SPACE in Rennes, France, from 15–17 September 2026. All 6 have been credited with 1 Innov’SPACE star.

Vincent ter BeekEditor of Pig Progress / Topic: Pigs around the world

Jul 13

Article

This year, 32 innovations at SPACE will receive an Innov’SPACE award. Photo: SPACE

The jury selected 32 innovations for an award this year. Whilst 26 received 1 star, 6 of them were awarded 2 stars. The jury’s top choices will be revealed at an Innov’SPACE event during the show, on 15 September.

The following pig innovations can be found at the event:

Precision feeding system for sows ⭐

Asserva: Materneo 4.0

Materneo 4.0 is a precision feeding system for sows in maternity pens, developed by French livestock equipment company Asserva. The system replaces theoretical feed plans with dynamic, individual adjustments. The feeding is controlled in real time based on the sow’s behaviour, actual consumption and intake capacity. The system integrates automated piglet weighing to measure growth rates. This weight change of the litter serves as a direct indicator to correct nutritional inputs before visible losses occur. The system combines 3 modules:

individualised distribution in fractionated doses;

animal performance measurement; and

a nutritional algorithm.

The objective is twofold: maximising the piglets’ ADG during this critical phase, and preserving the sow’s body condition to optimise recovery and future reproductive performance. Each pen becomes an autonomous decision-making unit driven by data. (Hall 8, A21)

Artificial insemination dummy for boar semen collection ⭐

Yxia: Mounting dummy

The boar mounting dummy is an essential tool for collecting semen for artificial insemination. The purpose of this product is to allow the animal to position itself comfortably on it while keeping its penis accessible for stimulation and collection. This dummy stands out for its structure, as it was designed for optimal cleaning thanks to its easily accessible parts. It also features a single, angled, off-centre stand to optimise space around the animal’s penis and facilitate the operator’s movements. In terms of materials, it is made of stainless steel – robust and highly corrosion-resistant – and its top surface is made of corian, a combination of resin and mineral powder. The latter is non-porous and impact-resistant. It is the combination of these innovative features that allows for the design of an ergonomic and durable mannequin, while reducing the risk of bacterial contamination during semen collection. (Hall 8, B32)

Automatic detection of lesions and sex of pig carcasses ⭐

Unissia – Pig’Scan

Pig’Scan is an automated, AI-powered system for analysing pig carcasses, designed to monitor animal welfare, tail biting, and health indicators in slaughterhouses. It performs synchronised multi-view acquisition (back, head and tail area) to automatically quantify tail length, tail biting lesions, dorsal scratches, signs of fighting, transport-related injuries and certain potential indicators of animal abuse. Thanks to its machine vision algorithms and RFID traceability, it transforms previously subjective observations into statistically usable digital data. Analysing the carcass backs identifies scratches, impacts and lesions that may reveal problems related to stress, transport, animal density or husbandry practices. It provides standardised and reproducible measurements, automatic archiving of visual evidence, a reduction in manual monitoring workload. (Hall 8, A 23a)

Simplifying deadstock management ⭐

Maison Bleue: Sanihouse

Sanihouse, designed by Innovlap and Maison Bleue, is a facility dedicated to managing deceased animals on farms. The facility is a structure made of insulated, precast reinforced concrete elements, featuring an assembly that includes a stainless-steel platform and automated, motorised gates. It makes the removal of deadstock both easier and safer. The platform’s motorisation is synchronised with the opening of the gates and is sized to hold rendering bins. These bins are automatically moved either to the “farming zone” side for loading carcasses, or to the “public zone” side, allowing the rendering service to collect them in complete autonomy. By isolating carcasses in an insulated facility equipped for easy cleaning and disinfection, ensuring independent access for the rendering service without entering the farm perimeter, and reducing handling, the system guarantees compliance with health regulations while simplifying deadstock management for farmers. (Outdoor, E21)

Automatic piglet weight estimation⭐

IMV Technologies: Smart Weight

Smart Weight is an add-on feature to the Smart Counting platform, designed for piglets up to 10 kg leaving the farrowing unit. As animals move through the counting alley, the system automatically tracks them across multiple images, aggregates visual measurements and estimates the lot’s average weight and weight dispersion without manual weighing or a detour to a truck scale. A simple user calibration is sufficient, with no on-site installation and no specific preventive maintenance. That way a counter becomes an objective production-management tool: immediate, time-stamped data that can be integrated into farm-management software. It brings precision, consistency and a new uniformity indicator. Economically, it reduces collection time, transport costs and supports a recurring subscription model. (Hall 1, G76)

Nutrition-environment assessment tool for the livestock industry ⭐

IFIP: Foodbasket7

The French Institute for Pork and Pig Research (IFIP) will launch Foodbasket7 at SPACE: a free web-based tool that allows members of the public to gain insight into the consequences of their dietary choices. They can create a weekly food basket in just a few minutes and the tool will then provide a simple and visual assessment of 2 complementary dimensions:

the nutritional quality of the food, through coverage of 28 essential nutrients (proteins, vitamins, minerals, fatty acids) defined according to the user’s profile (age, sex, and physical activity);

the environmental impacts of the food basket, such as greenhouse gas emissions, water consumption, land use, etc.

Its innovation lies in its comprehensive approach, which allows for reasoning at the scale of a complete diet rather than on an individual food item basis. For livestock sectors, Foodbasket7 offers a scientific analysis framework that allows for valuing the contribution of animal products to a healthy, diverse and sustainable diet by integrating the nutritional density of the food. This tool is available free of charge on the IFIP website. (Hall 8, A23a)
author: Vincent ter Beek
